title: Shared license-clean synthetic cancer fixtures
context: Each tutorial currently generates or borrows small synthetic data independently, leading to duplication of effort and inconsistent data quality. A shared, license-clean synthetic cancer fixture set — coordinated with the synthetic-cancer-data portfolio project — reduces the data-licensing surface for every future tutorial and makes fixture quality a shared responsibility rather than a per-tutorial ad-hoc decision. Fixtures are generated (not derived from real patient data), small, and unambiguously license-clean. This is Elyos-side tooling.
objective: Build and commit a shared set of license-clean synthetic cancer data fixtures (MAF, expression matrix, survival table, image patch, etc.) usable across tutorials for genomics, survival-analysis, and imaging contributions, coordinated with the synthetic-cancer-data portfolio project.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three fixture types are produced covering the ecosystems used in prior contributions: e.g., a small synthetic MAF file (genomics), a synthetic survival/censoring table (survival-analysis), and a synthetic image patch or feature matrix (imaging).
All fixtures are synthetically generated — no derived from real patient data; the generation script is committed so fixtures can be reproduced.
Fixtures are documented with: generation method, intended use cases, and an explicit 'no real patient data' statement.
Fixtures are MIT or CC0-licensed (unambiguously license-clean for redistribution).
Coordination with the synthetic-cancer-data portfolio project is documented (any shared decisions or handoffs noted).
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint are green; DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md — Data-minimization priority order (synthetic data tier)
output: A committed set of shared MIT/CC0-licensed synthetic cancer data fixtures covering at least three fixture types, with generation scripts and documentation, reducing data-licensing surface for all future tutorial contributions.
